steps: Fry rempah paste with dried shrimp until fragrant; add tamarind juice sugar and water; toss drained bee hoon in sauce; serve topped with sliced tau pok egg bean sprouts chives; squeeze lime
healthy_brands_and_where_to_buy: Bee hoon: FairPrice Rice Vermicelli (~.50) | Paste: Prima Taste Mee Siam Paste (~.50) | Tamarind: FairPrice Tamarind Paste (~.50) | Tau pok: FairPrice fried tofu puffs (~.50)

healthier_alternative: Sup Ayam (Chicken Soup with wholemeal bread)
ingredients: 2 chicken drumsticks (skinless); 1L water; 1 cinnamon stick; 2 star anise; 3 cloves; 1 onion; 2 potatoes (cubed); 1 carrot (cubed); 1 celery stalk; salt and pepper; fried shallots; 2 slices wholemeal bread
steps: Boil water with spices and onion 5 min; add chicken and simmer 20 min; add potato and carrot last 10 min; remove chicken and shred meat off bone; return meat to pot; season with salt pepper; serve in bowl with celery and fried shallots; eat with wholemeal bread
healthy_brands_and_where_to_buy: Chicken: SG Fresh skinless drumsticks (~.50) | Spices: MDH whole spices from Mustafa (~) | Vegetables: Fresh potato/carrot/celery (~) | Bread: Gardenia Wholemeal Bread (~)

original_dish: Dim Sum (Har Gow + Siew Mai + Char Siew Bao = 6pcs)
healthier_alternative: Steamed Dim Sum (Crystal Dumplings + Chee Cheong Fun)
ingredients: Order at restaurant: 2 crystal shrimp dumplings + 2 steamed siew mai + 1 plate chee cheong fun; skip char siew bao and fried items; have Chinese tea (pu-erh) for digestion
steps: Choose steamed over fried/baked; crystal dumplings are lighter than har gow; pick chee cheong fun (rice rolls) as a filler; drink Chinese tea; limit to 6-8 pieces total; skip sweet sauce go for light soy
healthy_brands_and_where_to_buy: Rice flour: FairPrice Rice Roll Flour (~) | Dim sum: FairPrice Frozen Steamed Dim Sum (~-8) | Tea: Chinese pu-erh from Chinatown tea shops (~) | N/A

ingredients: 200g mee pok noodles; 80g lean pork mince; 6 fishballs; 800ml pork/ikan bilis stock; 1 tbsp fried shallots; chye sim; light soy; vinegar (1 tsp on side); white pepper
steps: Boil stock; season pork mince with dash of soy and pepper; cook mee pok 1-2 min; form small pork meatballs or just drop mince into broth; add fishballs; add chye sim last; serve in bowl with broth and fried shallots; put vinegar on side
healthy_brands_and_where_to_buy: Noodles: Fresh mee pok from wet market (~.20) | Pork: FairPrice Lean Minced Pork (~.50) | Fishballs: FairPrice Fresh Fishballs (~.50) | Soy: Kwong Cheong Thye Light Soy (~)

ingredients: 200g lean pork mince; 100g prawns (chopped); 50g water chestnuts (diced); 1 tsp five-spice powder; 1 egg; 1 tbsp cornstarch; beancurd skin sheets; salt and pepper; sweet sauce for dipping
steps: Mix pork prawns water chestnuts five-spice egg cornstarch salt pepper; lay beancurd skin flat; spread mixture along edge and roll up tightly; steam rolls 12-15 min; optional: lightly pan-sear after for crispiness; slice and serve with sweet sauce
healthy_brands_and_where_to_buy: Pork: FairPrice Lean Minced Pork (~.50) | Prawns: Fresh from wet market (~) | Beancurd skin: FairPrice Tau Kee Sheets (~) | Spices: FairPrice Five-Spice Powder (~)

ingredients: Dough: 2 cups pau flour + 1 tsp instant yeast + 2 tbsp sugar + 100ml warm water + 1 tbsp oil; Filling: 150g chicken breast (diced) + 1 tbsp oyster sauce + 1 tsp sesame oil + 50g bamboo shoots + spring onion
steps: Make dough: mix flour yeast sugar water oil knead 5 min rest 30 min; make filling: dice chicken mix with oyster sauce sesame oil bamboo shoots; divide dough into 4; flatten wrap filling and pleat shut; steam on parchment 12-15 min
healthy_brands_and_where_to_buy: Flour: Prima Pau Flour (~) | Chicken: SG Fresh chicken breast (~.50) | Sauce: Lee Kum Kee Oyster Sauce (~) | Bamboo shoots: FairPrice Canned Bamboo Shoots (~)

ingredients: 100g pearl barley; 1.5L water; 2 pandan leaves; 1 tbsp rock sugar (or less); optional: soy milk splash
steps: Rinse barley well; boil in water with pandan leaves 40-45 min until soft; add rock sugar last 5 min (use half of what recipes say); cool and refrigerate; serve cold; barley grains are edible and nutritious

ingredients: Buy 1 fresh young coconut from fruit stall; ask them to chop open; drink water straight; scoop flesh for bonus fibre
steps: Buy from wet market or supermarket; drink directly; coconut water has natural potassium and electrolytes; no processing needed
